final_message:
Done. The refactor is complete and all 4 existing tests pass unmodified.

What changed:

- **`reportgen/config.py`** — replaced the `DEFAULTS` dict with a frozen dataclass `Config` carrying the same four typed fields and defaults (`title: str = "Report"`, `output_format: str = "text"`, `max_rows: int = 10`, `include_summary: bool = True`). `load_config(path)` now validates the JSON keys against `dataclasses.fields(Config)` and raises `ValueError: Unknown config key: '<key>'` for anything unrecognized, then returns `Config(**raw)` so file values override defaults exactly as before.
- **`reportgen/formatter.py`** and **`reportgen/loader.py`** — switched all config subscripting to attribute access (`config.output_format`, `config.title`, `config.include_summary`, `config.max_rows`). A grep confirms no `cfg[...]`/`config[...]` remains anywhere in `reportgen/`.
- `main.py` and `__init__.py` needed no changes since they only pass the config through.

Verified: `python3 -m pytest -q` passes (4 passed), and I spot-checked the new behavior directly — an unknown key raises `ValueError` naming it, overrides merge with defaults correctly, and assigning to a field raises `FrozenInstanceError`. No commits were made and nothing under `tests/` was touched.
